subject: All Classes
assignment_purpose: Render reusable classroom tools based on uploaded Mr. Friess worksheet references.

kwhl_chart.title: KWHL Chart
kwhl_chart.subtitle: Know — Want to know — How will I find out — Learned
kwhl_chart.meta: Careers 8 · All classes · Reusable thinking tool
kwhl_chart.directions: Before: Fill in K and W. During: Add to H as you discover sources or strategies. After: Complete the L column.
checklist: K column was filled in before the lesson started.
L column is in my own words.
I noted any W questions that were not answered.

fishbone_diagram.title: Fishbone Diagram
fishbone_diagram.subtitle: Identify the causes that lead to a problem, event, or outcome.
fishbone_diagram.directions: Write the problem or effect in the box. Label each cause category, then fill in specific causes.
fishbone_diagram.tip: Causes are not always obvious. Ask why this happened for each cause you write.
checklist: The Problem / Effect box is filled in.
Each category has at least 2 specific causes.
I identified the root cause and explained why it matters most.

sentence_frame_card.title: Sentence Frame Card
sentence_frame_card.subtitle: Use these frames to help explain thinking, support discussion, and write responses.

choice_board.title: Choice Board
choice_board.subtitle: Pick your tasks. Show what you know in the way that works best for you.
choice_board.directions: Choose 3 tasks that connect like a line — across, down, or diagonal. Check the box when you finish each one.
checklist: I completed 3 tasks in a row.
I checked the box on each finished task.
I can explain what I did to my teacher or partner.

scaffolded_quiz.title: Unit 2 Quiz
scaffolded_quiz.subtitle: Personality, Science & the Limits of Labels — Careers 8
scaffolded_quiz.support_label: Scaffolded Support — word bank & hints provided

multiple_choice.question: What is MBTI?
options: A personality framework based on 60+ years of peer-reviewed research
A personality test that sorts people into 16 types using 4 pairs of opposites
A five-trait spectrum model used to predict job performance
A government tool used to decide career pathways

multiple_choice.question: The Barnum Effect describes the tendency for people to
options: accept vague descriptions as personally meaningful

short_answer.question: Name one difference between MBTI and the Big Five. Explain why that difference matters.
short_answer.hint: Think about categories vs. spectrum or result stability.

short_answer.question: Explain what the Barnum Effect is and give one real-life example.
short_answer.hint: Think about horoscopes, personality tests, or fortune-cookie messages.
